data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 √ó THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing ‚Äî no G4 cross-child finding.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a file_impact = scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). DISJOINT from every sibling: outbound-applier cluster (33d0‚Üíapply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py; 2359‚Üíbatch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and schema/fixture/contract files (8937‚Üí_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; fe57‚Üí_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script; 59ef‚Üítest_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py). No sibling touches the probe script or test_roundtrip.py. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a AC1 round-trips comments, links, and parent ‚Äî NOT labels ‚Äî and asserts inbound‚Üîoutbound OUTCOMES, not the outbound `labels_applied` sub-op payload. The one real cross-child representation conflict in this epic (labels list-vs-count, correctly localized to the 33d0‚Üî2359 outbound-telemetry pairing) is therefore untouched by 822a. 822a's "production path" assumption is consistent with fe57 (unmassaged fixtures through fetch_snapshot), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (surviving production batch applier).
- Dependency gap ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency ‚Äî story A's fixtures/fake ‚Äî is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer‚Üîconsumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. 822a binds to "the production path" abstractly, so it needs no hard 33d0-before-822a edge: against current code the production batch path already carries the comment/link/parent dispatch its round-trip asserts (the epic closes a TEST/PROBE-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs), and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction ‚Äî the same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for keystone 8937.
- Scope overlap ‚Äî BENIGN. 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE = consolidation correctness), 8937 AC3 (assembled snapshot-entry CONTRACT through fetcher‚Üídiffer‚Üíapplier, test_snapshot_contract.py), and 822a AC1 (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound‚Üîoutbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the production path from distinct angles on distinct files ‚Äî complementary, not duplicative. 822a explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (‚Üí8937).
- Ordering violation ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0‚Üî2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster; the only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture and parent "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references ‚Äî BENIGN. The Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED guard 822a converts is its live audit target; no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references ‚Äî 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) ‚Äî not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent √ó 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
